key_questions: Perianal itch (especially nocturnal — hallmark: female worms lay eggs at night)?
Child in daycare/school (highly transmissible in children)?
Scotch tape test: apply sticky tape to perianal area at night, examine microscopically for eggs
red_flags: Significant weight loss, diarrhea, or malabsorption → other parasitic infection
Pelvic symptoms in girls → pinworm migration to vagina/uterus (requires gynaecology)

first_line.name: Mebendazole or Pyrantel Pamoate
first_line.drugs: Pyrantel pamoate (Combantrin) 11 mg/kg single dose (max 1g) — repeat in 2 weeks | Mebendazole 100 mg single dose — repeat in 2 weeks
first_line.notes: Single dose is effective — repeat in 2 weeks to kill worms hatched from eggs after first dose. TREAT THE ENTIRE HOUSEHOLD simultaneously (re-infection cycle). Pyrantel pamoate: OTC in Canada. Mebendazole: prescription. Both highly effective. Safe in children >1 year.

non_pharm: Treat ALL household members simultaneously — key to eradication
Wash all bedding, towels, underwear in hot water (day of treatment)
Trim fingernails short (eggs under nails are main transmission route)
Strict hand-washing especially after toilet use and before meals
Morning shower — removes eggs deposited overnight in perianal area
treatment.refer_when: Treatment failure after 2 courses; immunocompromised; ectopic infection (vaginal, peritoneal)
